name: Mata, Arenas de Iguña, Santiurde de Toranzo – circular route
name_original: Mata, Arenas de Iguña, Santiurde de Toranzo – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around San Felices De Buelna are set within Cantabria's diverse landscape, characterized by lush green areas, valleys, and mountains. The region offers varied terrain suitable for road cyclists, from challenging ascents to more relaxed rides through scenic rural paths. The topography includes numerous hills and expansive views, providing a dynamic environment for cycling. Best road cycling routes around San Felices De Buelna The most popular road cycling route is Mata, Arenas de Iguña, Santiurde de Toranzo – circular route, a 33.1 miles (53.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 57 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alto de La Montaña – Los Corrales de Buelna loop from Lombera, a moderate 23.4 miles (37.7 km) path. This route includes moderate climbs and offers scenic vistas over the surrounding area. Local road cyclists also love the Los Corrales de Buelna loop from Estación de Las Caldas de Besaya, a 30.3 miles (48.7 km) trail leading through green landscapes and valleys, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Road cycling around San Felices De Buelna is defined by green landscapes, valleys, and hills, offering a network of routes for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in San Felices De Buelna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 5000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore San Felices De Buelna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around San Felices De Buelna?
A: There are over 130 road cycling routes available around San Felices De Buelna. This includes a diverse range of options, with 17 easy routes, 70 moderate routes, and 45 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in San Felices De Buelna?
A: The terrain around San Felices De Buelna is quite varied, characterized by lush green landscapes, valleys, and mountains. You can expect a mix of challenging ascents and more relaxed rides through scenic rural paths, with numerous hills and expansive views providing a dynamic cycling experience.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in the area?
A: Yes, there are 17 easy road cycling routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39559205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Corrales de Buelna loop from Estación de Los Corrales de Buelna&lt;/a&gt;, which is 30.2 miles (48.6 km) long and features a manageable elevation gain, making it a great option for those new to road cycling.

Q: Can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs?
A: Absolutely. The region offers 45 difficult road cycling routes for those seeking a challenge. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004241123/mata-arenas-de-iguna-santiurde-de-toranzo-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mata, Arenas de Iguña, Santiurde de Toranzo – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, a 33.1-mile (53.2 km) trail with significant elevation gain through varied terrain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around San Felices De Buelna?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops, offering circular journeys. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38284905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Montaña – Los Corrales de Buelna loop from Lombera&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 23.4-mile (37.7 km) path that includes moderate climbs and scenic vistas.

Q: What natural features can I see while road cycling in San Felices De Buelna?
A: Road cycling routes often pass through beautiful natural landscapes. You might encounter features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4146541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Churrón de Borleña Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4370882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of Pico La Capía (Pico Dobra)&lt;/a&gt;. The region is known for its green valleys and hills.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. While cycling, you might pass by sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4847953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintana IV Tumulus&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5135765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Statue of Francis of Assisi&lt;/a&gt;. The 14th-century Pero Niño Tower in Sovilla also serves as a cultural center, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in San Felices De Buelna?
A: The road cycling routes in San Felices De Buelna are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful green landscapes, and the rewarding scenic views encountered along the paths.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in the area?
A: While specific dedicated family-friendly road cycling routes within San Felices de Buelna are not detailed, the broader Cantabrian region offers options like the Besaya valley greenway, which is flatter and suitable for intermediate cyclists. For a non-cycling family activity, the Senda Mitológica del Monte Tejas near Tarriba is a 2-kilometer circular trail with mythological sculptures.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in San Felices De Buelna?
A: The lush green landscapes suggest that spring and early autumn would be ideal, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can also be enjoyable, though it might be warmer, especially on routes with less shade. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: Yes, some routes or nearby areas feature waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4146541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Churrón de Borleña Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument in the vicinity that you might be able to incorporate into a ride or visit as a side trip.

Q: Are there cafes or pubs along the road cycling routes for a break?
A: While specific cafes or pubs directly on every route are not listed, San Felices De Buelna and its surrounding towns like Los Corrales de Buelna offer various establishments where cyclists can stop for refreshments. Planning your route to pass through these towns will provide opportunities for breaks.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists near the starting points of routes?
A: Many routes start from towns or accessible points where public parking is generally available. For example, routes often begin near Estación de Los Corrales de Buelna or Lombera, where you can typically find places to park your vehicle before starting your ride.
tips:
This church is located in Vejorís de Toranzo, located on a hill where it stands out from the center of the town.
Its construction is from the 16th century, its Renaissance portal with three naves and fluted shaft columns. Inside, there is a Renaissance main altarpiece, with three sections and carved panels with the Annunciation, Visitation and Three Wise Men, and this is where the exceptional enamel altarpiece comes from, a jewel that is currently preserved in the Santillana Diocesan Museum.

Also known as El Puente Rual, the aqueduct is next to the Vía Verde del Pas. It was built in 1885 in order to supply drinking water to the city of Santander. It is an ideal place to take a short break – there is shade, benches and tables. In front there is a bar and a fountain.

German-made locomotive that entered service in 1913 to transport iron ores and limestone from the Camargo mines to its factory. It is located on the greenway, near the train station, converted into an exhibition hall and tourist information office.
